text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Hawthorne, there lived a peculiar old gnome named Gerald. Gerald loved nothing more than wandering through meadows collecting wildflowers. He had a grand garden full of them. One day, Gerald was plucking a bright yellow flower when he stumbled upon the most astonishing object. It resembled a flower but it sparkled mysteriously—it was a butterfly made entirely of gold leaf!

Stupefied, Gerald leaned closer to get a better look. The butterfly fluttered its wings, emitting a soft, melodic song. It perched on a golden bough and seemed to hum its appreciation.

Excited, Gerald returned home and carefully crafted a little gold-flowered basket to keep the beautiful creature. Every day he'd sit quietly outside admiring it.

Then one quiet spring evening, a storm swept through. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and rain drummed against the windows. Through the mist, Gerald saw something strange—another butterfly, this one shining
